questions.question: The sudden ______ of the global economy after the pandemic sparked intense debates among scholars regarding the resilience of modern financial systems.

options.A: deterioration
options.B: fluctuation
options.C: contraction
options.D: augmentation
options.E: stabilization

questions.question: Despite the rigorous testing phase, some critics argued that the results were ______ by a lack of diverse demographic representation in the study group.

options.A: alleviated
options.B: compromised
options.C: enhanced
options.D: authenticated
options.E: validated

questions.question: Ancient Roman engineering is ______ characterized by its durability and innovation, as evidenced by the aqueducts that still stand today.

options.A: sporadically
options.B: negligibly
options.C: adversely
options.D: distinctively
options.E: marginally

questions.question: While the implementation of renewable energy sources has increased significantly over the last decade, ______.

options.A: fossil fuel consumption continues to dominate the global energy market
options.B: solar panels are becoming more affordable for domestic use
options.C: government subsidies have encouraged many new startups
options.D: carbon emissions have dropped to pre-industrial levels
options.E: technological advancements are making storage easier

questions.question: Even though the psychological effects of social media are still being researched, ______.

options.A: most users find it beneficial for networking
options.B: it is clear that excessive use can lead to anxiety
options.C: it was invented for communication purposes
options.D: digital literacy is taught in primary schools
options.E: companies are spending more on online ads

questions.question: No sooner had the archaeologists discovered the hidden tomb ______.

options.A: when they realized it had already been looted
options.B: than they alerted the local authorities
options.C: as the weather conditions began to worsen
options.D: that the museum offered to house the relics
options.E: where the gold coins were scattered

questions.question: Because the urban population is growing at an unprecedented rate, ______.

options.A: city planners are struggling to provide adequate housing
options.B: rural areas are becoming more densely populated
options.C: migration patterns are shifting toward coastal towns
options.D: agricultural productivity has doubled recently
options.E: transportation costs are surprisingly falling

questions.question: CLOZE TEXT: The Enlightenment was an intellectual movement that (5) ______ the 17th and 18th centuries. Question 5:

options.A: preceded
options.B: spanned
options.C: isolated
options.D: diverted
options.E: refuted

questions.question: CLOZE TEXT: (6) ______ its focus on reason, it challenged traditional authority. Question 6:

options.A: Instead of
options.B: In spite of
options.C: Due to
options.D: Prior to
options.E: Rather than

questions.question: CLOZE TEXT: Thinkers like Locke and Voltaire argued (7) ______ individual liberty. Question 7:

options.A: against
options.B: for
options.C: with
options.D: into
options.E: onto

questions.question: CLOZE TEXT: These ideas (8) ______ influenced the American Revolution. Question 8:

options.A: scarcely
options.B: vaguely
options.C: profoundly
options.D: erroneously
options.E: superficially

questions.question: CLOZE TEXT: (9) ______ its historical significance, many modern democracies still uphold these values. Question 9:

options.A: Regardless of
options.B: Given
options.C: By means of
options.D: Except for
options.E: As if

questions.question: Küresel ısınma, kutup buzullarının erimesine ve deniz seviyelerinin yükselmesine neden olmaktadır.

options.A: Global warming causes polar ice caps to melt and sea levels to rise.
options.B: The melting of ice caps is caused by rising sea levels and global warming.
options.C: Global warming is caused by rising sea levels and melting ice.
options.D: Polar ice caps melt when global warming causes sea levels to rise.
options.E: Sea levels rise because global warming melts the polar ice.

questions.question: Sanayi Devrimi, üretim yöntemlerini kökten değiştirerek modern toplumun temelini atmıştır.

options.A: The Industrial Revolution changed production methods to create modern society.
options.B: The foundation of modern society was changed by the Industrial Revolution.
options.C: By radically changing production methods, the Industrial Revolution laid the foundation of modern society.
options.D: Production methods were changed radically because the Industrial Revolution was the foundation.
options.E: Modern society founded the Industrial Revolution through production methods.

questions.question: Recent studies suggest that sleep deprivation significantly impairs cognitive functions.

options.A: Son çalışmalar, uykusuzluğun bilişsel fonksiyonları önemli ölçüde bozduğunu göstermektedir.
options.B: Uykusuzluk bilişsel fonksiyonları bozduğu için son çalışmalar yapılmıştır.
options.C: Bilişsel fonksiyonlar uykusuzluktan etkilendiği için çalışmalar sürmektedir.
options.D: Uykusuzluğun bilişsel yetenekleri etkilediği son çalışmalarda görülmüştür.
options.E: Çalışmaların çoğu uykusuzluğun zihinsel faaliyetleri durdurduğunu söyler.

questions.question: Language is not only a tool for communication but also a reflection of culture.

options.A: Dil sadece iletişim için bir araç değil, aynı zamanda kültürün bir yansımasıdır.
options.B: İletişim aracı olan dil kültürün kendisini yansıtır.
options.C: Kültür yansıtıldığı sürece dil bir iletişim aracıdır.
options.D: Dil ve kültür iletişim için kullanılan araçlardır.
options.E: İletişim aracı olarak kullanılan dil kültürle beraber gelişir.

questions.question: Unless the government intervenes immediately, the inflation rate will reach record highs by the end of the year.

options.A: The inflation rate will stay low if the government acts quickly.
options.B: If the government doesn't step in right away, inflation will be at an all-time high by year-end.
options.C: Despite government intervention, inflation will likely rise by the end of the year.
options.D: The government must intervene to stop inflation from reaching record levels last year.
options.E: Record high inflation is expected regardless of what the government does.

questions.question: Few people realize that the Sahara was once a lush green landscape with abundant wildlife.

options.A: Many people know that the Sahara used to be green and full of animals.
options.B: The Sahara is currently a green area but people don't realize it.
options.C: It is not widely known that the Sahara was formerly a fertile region with many animals.
options.D: The Sahara has always been a desert despite what some people believe.
options.E: Archaeologists discovered that the Sahara was green, but nobody cares.

questions.question: The manager postponed the meeting until Friday because several team members were ill.

options.A: The meeting was moved to Friday due to the illness of some staff.
options.B: The manager ill-treated the team members during the Friday meeting.
options.C: Since everyone was sick, the meeting on Friday was cancelled.
options.D: Postponing the meeting until Friday made the team members feel ill.
options.E: Friday's meeting was delayed although the team members were healthy.

questions.question: It is estimated that artificial intelligence will create more jobs than it destroys in the long run.

options.A: AI is expected to lead to a net increase in employment over time.
options.B: AI will definitely destroy all current jobs in the future.
options.C: Long-term job loss is the most likely outcome of AI development.
options.D: Experts think AI will destroy jobs faster than it creates them.
options.E: The number of jobs created by AI is equal to the number of jobs it destroys.

questions.question:
John: Have you seen the latest report on urban heat islands? 
Mark: Yes, it's quite alarming how much higher temperatures are in cities. 
John: ______ 
Mark: Exactly. More trees and green roofs could make a significant difference.

options.A: I think we should move to the countryside.
options.B: It's mainly because of the lack of vegetation and the abundance of concrete.
options.C: Air conditioning is making the problem worse.
options.D: The report didn't offer any solutions though.
options.E: I wonder if the government will increase taxes.

questions.question:
Susan: I'm struggling to choose between a degree in literature or linguistics. 
Academic Advisor: Both are great, but they offer very different perspectives. 
Susan: ______ 
Academic Advisor: Literature focuses on the artistic side, while linguistics is more scientific and analytical.

options.A: Can I study both at the same time?
options.B: What do you mean by different perspectives?
options.C: I've always loved reading novels.
options.D: Will I be able to find a job easily?
options.E: Which one do you recommend for a teacher?

questions.question:
Patient: I've been feeling very lethargic lately, even though I sleep well. 
Doctor: It could be due to a variety of factors, including your diet or iron levels. 
Patient: ______ 
Doctor: Then we should definitely run some blood tests to be sure.

options.A: I eat very healthily and take vitamins.
options.B: I've always been a tired person.
options.C: My sister has the same problem.
options.D: I haven't changed my diet recently.
options.E: I suspect it might be my work stress.

questions.question:
Tourist: Is it possible to see the entire museum in just one afternoon? 
Guide: To be honest, it's quite vast. You might have to rush. 
Tourist: ______ 
Guide: In that case, I suggest focusing on the Egyptian and Renaissance wings.

options.A: I have all the time in the world.
options.B: I only want to see the highlights.
options.C: Is there a cafeteria inside?
options.D: How much is the entrance fee?
options.E: Can I take photos of the statues?

questions.question: Genetic engineering has the potential to solve many global issues, from food security to genetic diseases. However, it also raises ethical concerns. ______. Critics worry about the long-term effects on biodiversity and the possibility of creating 'designer babies'.

options.A: Scientists are excited about the new possibilities.
options.B: The debate over its safety is still ongoing.
options.C: Many countries have already banned GMOs.
options.D: Ethical guidelines are being developed by international bodies.
options.E: For instance, the manipulation of human embryos is highly controversial.

questions.question: In the world of finance, 'liquidity' refers to how quickly an asset can be converted into cash without affecting its market price. Cash is considered the most liquid asset. ______. Real estate, on the other hand, is relatively illiquid because it takes time to sell.

options.A: Stocks and bonds are also highly liquid.
options.B: Gold has always been a safe investment.
options.C: Banks require a lot of cash to operate.
options.D: Interest rates affect the liquidity of the market.
options.E: Investors prefer liquid assets during a crisis.

questions.question: The Great Wall of China is not a single continuous wall but a series of fortifications built over centuries. ______. It was primarily designed to protect the Chinese Empire from nomadic tribes from the north.

options.A: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.
options.B: Construction began as early as the 7th century BC.
options.C: The wall is visible from the moon with the naked eye.
options.D: Many workers died during its construction.
options.E: It stretches over 13,000 miles across the country.

questions.question: Renewable energy is often criticized for its intermittency, as solar and wind power depend on weather conditions. ______. These technologies allow excess energy to be stored and used when the sun isn't shining or the wind isn't blowing.

options.A: However, advancements in battery technology are addressing this issue.
options.B: Fossil fuels are more reliable in this regard.
options.C: Governments are still subsidizing coal mines.
options.D: Energy consumption is highest during the winter months.
options.E: Nuclear power is a cleaner alternative to wind.

questions.question: It is virtually impossible to predict the exact outcome of the election at this stage.

options.A: The election result is almost certain.
options.B: We can easily guess who will win the election.
options.C: It is nearly impossible to foresee the result of the election now.
options.D: The outcome of the election will be announced very soon.
options.E: Nobody wants to predict the election because it is difficult.

questions.question: Hardly had the movie started when the fire alarm went off.

options.A: The movie started after the fire alarm went off.
options.B: The fire alarm rang just as the movie was beginning.
options.C: The movie was cancelled because of a fire alarm.
options.D: They waited for the fire alarm before starting the movie.
options.E: The movie finished right before the fire alarm rang.

questions.question: The team would have won the match if the captain hadn't been injured.

options.A: The team won despite the captain's injury.
options.B: The team lost because the captain was injured.
options.C: The captain's injury didn't affect the match result.
options.D: The team didn't win even though the captain was healthy.
options.E: The captain was injured but the team still won.

questions.question: (I) Quantum mechanics is a fundamental theory in physics that describes the physical properties of nature at the scale of atoms. (II) It is the foundation of all quantum physics including quantum chemistry and quantum technology. (III) Most people find it very difficult to understand because it is counter-intuitive. (IV) Albert Einstein was born in Germany in 1879. (V) Despite its complexity, it has led to the development of transistors and lasers.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Caffeine is a central nervous system stimulant of the methylxanthine class. (II) It is the world's most widely consumed psychoactive substance. (III) Coffee beans are actually the seeds of a fruit called a coffee cherry. (IV) Unlike many other psychoactive substances, it is legal and unregulated in nearly all parts of the world. (V) It works by blocking the action of adenosine, a neurotransmitter that promotes sleep.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic 'rebirth' following the Middle Ages. (II) It took place from the 14th century to the 17th century. (III) During this time, many classical Greek and Roman texts were rediscovered. (IV) The Black Death killed nearly half of the population of Europe in the 14th century. (V) This period produced some of the greatest artists in history, such as Leonardo da Vinci.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: The Amazon Rainforest is often referred to as the 'lungs of the planet' because its vegetation continuously recycles carbon dioxide into oxygen. However, this vast ecosystem is under threat from deforestation, primarily driven by cattle ranching and soy production. As trees are cleared, the forest's ability to absorb carbon diminishes, accelerating global warming. QUESTION: Why is the Amazon Rainforest called the 'lungs of the planet'?

options.A: Because it is the largest forest in the world.
options.B: Because it produces a large amount of oxygen by recycling carbon dioxide.
options.C: Because it is shaped like a human lung from above.
options.D: Because it provides a habitat for many different species.
options.E: Because it is located in the center of South America.

questions.question: PASSAGE 1 (cont.): The Amazon Rainforest... QUESTION: What is the main cause of deforestation mentioned in the text?

options.A: Urbanization and road construction.
options.B: Mining and logging.
options.C: Cattle ranching and soy production.
options.D: Forest fires caused by lightning.
options.E: Illegal hunting and poaching.

questions.question: PASSAGE 1 (cont.): The Amazon Rainforest... QUESTION: According to the passage, how does deforestation affect global warming?

options.A: It causes more rainfall in the region.
options.B: It reduces the forest's ability to absorb carbon dioxide.
options.C: It leads to a decrease in the Earth's temperature.
options.D: It has no direct impact on the climate.
options.E: It increases the number of cattle in the world.

questions.question: Architecture in Ancient Greece is best known for its temples, many of which are found throughout the region, mostly as ruins but many substantially intact. The second important type of building that survives is the open-air theater. The formal vocabulary of ancient Greek architecture, in particular the division of architectural style into three defined orders: the Doric, the Ionic and the Corinthian, was to have a profound effect on Western architecture of later periods. QUESTION: What are the two main types of buildings surviving from Ancient Greece mentioned in the text?

options.A: Palaces and libraries.
options.B: Temples and open-air theaters.
options.C: Aqueducts and stadiums.
options.D: Markets and city walls.
options.E: Schools and houses.

questions.question: PASSAGE 2 (cont.): Architecture in Ancient Greece... QUESTION: Which of the following is NOT one of the three architectural orders mentioned?

options.A: Doric
options.B: Ionic
options.C: Corinthian
options.D: Byzantine
options.E: None of the above

questions.question: PASSAGE 2 (cont.): Architecture in Ancient Greece... QUESTION: What effect did Ancient Greek architecture have on later periods according to the text?

options.A: It had no impact on Western design.
options.B: It influenced the formal vocabulary of Western architecture.
options.C: It was quickly forgotten after the Roman Empire.
options.D: It was only used for religious buildings.
options.E: It caused people to stop building theaters.
